name: dvp-happy-path
description: Watch a compliance-gated atomic settlement happen end-to-end on a Solana sandbox. Two participants are registered + verified in the on-chain compliance registry; a 2-leg DvP is funded; the wrapper verifies both sides on-chain before signing the SettleDvp CPI. Useful for asking: how does on-chain compliance enforcement at the *composition* layer (not as a Token-2022 transfer hook) behave under stress? What changes if you flip the order of register/verify? What if a participant is suspended mid-flow?
headline: Two verified participants atomically swap asset ↔ cash; on-chain compliance gate fires before settlement.

steps.explanation: Initialize the on-chain compliance registry. The signer becomes the initial super-admin.
Register the first participant. Status starts at PENDING.
Verify the first participant. Status moves PENDING → VERIFIED.

(Off-CLI) Use spl-token-cli to create two Token-2022 mints (asset + cash) without TransferHook, then mint inventory to USER_A (asset) and USER_B (cash). The wrapper handles compliance at composition; the mints don't need to.
Print the wrapper's settlement-authority PDA. No RPC — pure derivation.
Compute the SwapDvp PDA the SPC program will create.
(Off-CLI) Use any SPC client to call SPC CreateDvp permissionlessly with the wrapper PDA as `settlement_authority`. Then both parties fund their escrow leg via SPL Transfer.
steps.command: # (SPC CreateDvp call — outside ssr-cli; see SPC's docs)

steps.explanation: Settle. The wrapper reads user_a + user_b from the SwapDvp account, verifies both AccountRecord PDAs (status VERIFIED), signs SettleDvp as the wrapper PDA, and SPC atomically delivers asset ↔ cash + closes the SwapDvp + escrow ATAs.
